# Data Attribute Assignment
This section summarizes how user uploaded data or default data variables were assigned to the standard
variables used in the tool during the initial load processing. Each table below includes information on how
the original/user uploaded data variables relate to the standard variables, as well as the total count and
proportion of of each variable.

```{python}
from report_utils import stats_utils
from report_utils.render_guard import render_guard
from report_utils.table_utils import (
    format_count,
    format_currency,
    format_percent,
    format_miles,
    render_latex_table,
)

```

## Crash Variables
### Crash Severity
The table below consolidates every raw severity label that maps to the same
standardized severity class and reports the observed counts and proportions for
the study.

```{python}
# | output: asis
with render_guard("Crash severity data not available for this study."):
    severity_df = stats_utils.get_crash_severity_dataframe(study_id)
    render_latex_table(
        severity_df,
        {
            "Total Count": format_count,
            "Proportion": format_percent,
        },
    )
```

### Crash Costs
```{python}
# | output: asis
with render_guard("Crash cost data not available for this study."):
    crash_cost_df = stats_utils.get_crash_cost_dataframe(study_id)
    render_latex_table(
        crash_cost_df,
        {
            "Crash Cost": format_currency,
            "Total Count": format_count,
            "Proportion": format_percent,
        },
    )
```

### Crash Mode
```{python}
# | output: asis
with render_guard("Crash mode data not available for this study."):
    mode_df = stats_utils.get_crash_mode_dataframe(study_id)
    render_latex_table(
        mode_df,
        {
            "Total Count": format_count,
            "Proportion": format_percent,
        },
    )
```

## Road Variables
### Road Functional Classification
```{python}
# | output: asis
with render_guard("Road functional classification data not available for this study."):
    fclass_df = stats_utils.get_functional_class_dataframe(study_id)
    render_latex_table(
        fclass_df,
        {
            "Total Miles": format_miles,
            "Proportion": format_percent,
        },
    )
```
